From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American engineer and inventor

| Sherm Poppen | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | Sherman Robert Poppen  (1930-03-25)March 25, 1930  [Muskegon, Michigan](/wiki/Muskegon,_Michigan "Muskegon, Michigan"), U.S. |
| Died | July 31, 2019(2019-07-31) (aged 89)  [Griffin, Georgia](/wiki/Griffin,_Georgia "Griffin, Georgia"), U.S. |
| Occupations | Engineer, inventor |
| Spouse(s) | Nancy Bazarnick  ​ (died. 1993)​ [[1]](#cite_note-a-1) Louise Kelly[[1]](#cite_note-a-1) |

**Sherman Robert Poppen** (March 25, 1930 – July 31, 2019), also known as **Sherm Poppen** and **Sherman Poppen**, was an American [engineer](/wiki/Engineer "Engineer") and inventor. He was known for inventing [snowboarding](/wiki/Snowboarding "Snowboarding") in 1965.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Life and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sherm_Poppen&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life and career")]

Poppen was born in [Muskegon, Michigan](/wiki/Muskegon,_Michigan "Muskegon, Michigan"). He attended [Northwestern University](/wiki/Northwestern_University "Northwestern University"), earning a [bachelor's degree](/wiki/Bachelor%27s_degree "Bachelor's degree") in business.[[1]](#cite_note-a-1) He was a supply officer in the [navy](/wiki/Navy "Navy").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Poppen was an [industrial gases](/wiki/Industrial_gas "Industrial gas") [engineer](/wiki/Engineer "Engineer").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In 1965, Poppen invented a toy for his daughters by lashing a pair of 36-inch wooden snow skis side by side.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The toy was named the [snurfer](/wiki/Snurfer "Snurfer").[[6]](#cite_note-6) It gained popularity and the rights was given to [Brunswick Corporation](/wiki/Brunswick_Corporation "Brunswick Corporation"),[[1]](#cite_note-a-1) a manufacturing company.

Poppen died on July 31, 2019, at his home in [Griffin, Georgia](/wiki/Griffin,_Georgia "Griffin, Georgia"),[[7]](#cite_note-7) at the age of 89.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)
